oligos.name: Tn5 ME oligo
oligos.role: Mosaic-end (ME) bottom strand of the transposase adapter; anneals to both the barcoded Tn5 primers (P5-side adaptor) and the Tn5 primer C oligo (P7-side adaptor)
oligos.notes: 5' phosphorylated. Common ME strand loaded into every transposome; complementary to the AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG ME carried by the other adapter strands.

oligos.name: Tn5 primer C oligo
oligos.role: Non-barcoded transposase P7-side adapter; anneals with Tn5 ME oligo to form the P7 arm of the transposome

components.name: Tn5 primer C handle (P7 landing)
components.role: constant handle; landing site for the indexed MGI P7 primer

oligos.notes: P7-side adaptor of the transposome dimer.

oligos.name: 1st Tn5 Barcode primer (384-plex, first-round index)
oligos.role: Barcoded transposase P5-side adapter; introduces the first round of single-nucleus indexing during tagmentation

components.name: SMART/HY handle (constant)
components.role: constant handle; hybridization target for the 2nd-round barcoded HY oligo and the block-tail primer

oligos.notes: 384 distinct barcodes (Tn5_barcode_primer_1..384). 5' phosphorothioate bond (written A*A in Table S1). Example primer 1 barcode = TCCTACCAGT; barcodes are 10 bp. Annealed to the phosphorylated Tn5 ME oligo before transposome assembly.

oligos.name: HY_head_oligo
oligos.role: Short oligo that anneals to the 3' end of the barcoded HY oligo; provides the P5-landing region + ME and completes the second-round hybridization primer duplex

components.name: P5 landing region
components.role: landing site for the PCR P5 primer 3' end

oligos.notes: Reverse complement of the 3' constant region of the Barcoded HY oligo (CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGCTGCCGACGA).

oligos.name: 2nd Hybridization primer / Barcoded HY oligo (768-plex, second-round index)
oligos.role: Hybridization barcode oligo; introduces the second round of indexing by hybridizing (ligase-free) to the Tn5 SMART handle on tagmented fragments

components.name: SMART-handle complement
components.role: reverse complement of AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT; hybridizes to the Tn5 barcoded adapter handle

components.name: ME complement + P5-landing complement
components.role: ME (CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT) plus GACGCTGCCGACGA; templates the P5-side read structure

oligos.notes: 768 distinct barcodes (Barcoded_HY_oligo_1..768). Barcode length is variable (~8-11 bp; example oligo 1 = TTTCTCGCATG, oligo 2 = TCCTACCAGT). Used as a duplex with HY_head_oligo (preannealed 50 uM each).

oligos.name: Block_tail_primer_oligo
oligos.role: Blocking oligo added after hybridization to sequester free/redundant hybridization primers
oligos.notes: Identical to the Tn5 SMART handle; competes for free barcoded HY oligos to prevent cross-hybridization.

oligos.name: PCR P5 primer (MGI, library amplification)
oligos.role: Library-amplification primer that adds the MGI P5 adapter to the HY/P5 arm
oligos.notes: 5' phosphorylated. 3' end (TCGTCGGCAGCGTC) primes on the HY_head/P5-landing region.

oligos.name: 3rd MGI P7 indexed primer (96-plex provided; third-round index)
oligos.role: Library-amplification primer that adds the MGI P7 adapter and the i7 sample index on the Tn5-primer-C (P7) arm

components.name: Tn5 primer C landing
components.role: primes on the Tn5 primer C handle

oligos.notes: 96 indices provided in Table S1 (MGI_P7_index_1..96); the paper describes up to 384 indexed P7 adapters for the full assay. Index length 10 bp (example index 1 = TAGGTCCGAT).

final_library.source_label: CH-ATAC-seq final MGI library (5'->3' top strand)
annotation_lines: GAACGACATGGCTACGATCCGACTTTCGTCGGCAGCGTC = MGI P5 adapter (PCR P5 primer)
CCGAGCCCACGAGAC = Tn5 primer C handle (reverse complement of GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG)
GAAGACAACAACTCCTTGGCTCACA = MGI P7 adapter (reverse complement of TGTGAGCCAAGGAGTTGTTGTCTTC)

evidence.locator: CH-ATAC-seq final MGI library (5'->3' top strand)

library_generation.title: Transposome assembly (barcoded Tn5)
library_generation.summary: 384 uniquely indexed Tn5 transposomes are assembled, each loaded with a barcoded P5-side adapter and a common primer C P7-side adapter.
library_generation.note: For each well the Tn5 primer C oligo, Tn5 ME oligo and a barcoded Tn5 primer are annealed (95 C -> 10 C), then loaded onto Tn5 transposase (Vazyme S111) at 30 C. The ME oligo (5' phosphorylated) is the shared bottom strand of both adapter arms.
library_generation.product:
Barcoded (P5-side) adaptor:
5'- AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT[TN5_BARCODE:10]AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG -3'
                        3'-           TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC -5'   (Tn5 ME oligo, /5Phos/)

Primer C (P7-side) adaptor:
5'- GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG -3'
                     3'- TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC -5'   (Tn5 ME oligo, /5Phos/)

library_generation.title: Tagmentation (first-round index)
library_generation.summary: Nuclei split into 96-well plates are tagmented by well-specific barcoded Tn5, inserting the barcoded and primer C adapters into accessible chromatin.
library_generation.note: 23 uL nuclei + 2 uL indexed Tn5 per well, 55 C for 30 min. Tn5 leaves 9-bp gaps at each insertion junction (filled later). This introduces the first of three barcodes.
library_generation.product:
5'- AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT[TN5_BARCODE:10]AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[ genomic DNA ]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC -3'
3'-                        TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[ genomic DNA ]AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG                     -5'
                                   (9-bp gaps at both ME/genomic junctions)

library_generation.title: Oligo hybridization (second-round index)
library_generation.summary: A barcoded HY oligo (with HY_head oligo) hybridizes ligase-free to the Tn5 SMART handle, appending the second barcode; free primers are then blocked.
library_generation.note: Preannealed HY_head_oligo + barcoded HY oligo (25 uM) added per well, 37 C for 90 min; block-tail primer added to sequester redundant hybridization primers. The HY oligo SMART-complement (ACTCTGCGTTGATACCACTGCTT) anneals to AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT.
library_generation.product:
second-round barcoded HY oligo docks on the P5 arm handle:
5'- ...AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT[TN5_BARCODE:10]AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[genomic]... -3'
3'- ...ACTCTGCGTTGATACCACTGCTT-[HY_BARCODE:10]-AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC -5'  (barcoded HY oligo, 3'->5')

library_generation.title: PNK, gap-fill/ligation & nuclei lysis
library_generation.summary: T4 PNK phosphorylates ends; T4 DNA polymerase + Ampligase fill the 9-bp gaps and seal nicks; nuclei are lysed to release fully barcoded fragments.
library_generation.note: PNK 37 C 30 min; gap-fill mix (Ampligase + T4 DNA polymerase + dNTP) 37 C 60 min; proteinase K/SDS lysis 55 C 15 min, SDS quenched with Tween. Produces the intact double-stranded, dually barcoded fragment.

library_generation.title: Library PCR (third-round index)
library_generation.summary: The MGI P5 primer and an indexed MGI P7 primer amplify the fragment, adding the P5 adapter, the i7 sample index (third barcode) and the P7 adapter.
library_generation.note: KAPA HiFi, 72 C 5 min / 98 C 3 min / 12 cycles (98 C 10 s, 65 C 15 s, 72 C 1 min) / 72 C 5 min. P5 primer primes on TCGTCGGCAGCGTC; indexed P7 primer primes on GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG. Product = the final sequenceable amplicon.

library_generation.title: MGI circularization & DNB sequencing
library_generation.summary: The linear library is circularized to ssDNA, rolling-circle amplified into DNA nanoballs, and sequenced on MGI DNBSEQ-T7.
library_generation.note: VAHTS Circularization Kit for MGI + DNBSEQ DNB preparation kit. DNBs sequenced with custom Tn5-modified (TM) primers using a dark-reaction model (read1 100 cycles with dark cycles 11-33 and 44-62; read2 100 cycles).
library_generation.product: single-stranded circle -> DNA nanoball (DNB) carrying the step-5 amplicon; loaded onto the MGI patterned flow cell for combinatorial-index + genomic reads.

library_sequencing.note: Primer is identical to the P5-arm top strand (P5-landing + ME), so it anneals to the bottom strand and reads the top-strand sequence rightward. Imaged: HY barcode (cyc 1-10); dark reaction across the 23 nt SMART/HY handle (cyc 11-33); Tn5 barcode (cyc 34-43); dark reaction across the 19 nt ME (cyc 44-62); genomic open-chromatin insert (cyc 63-100).
library_sequencing.diagram:
pri  5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG------------------------------------------------->
top  5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[HY-BC x10]AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT[Tn5-BC x10]AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[==genomic==]-3'
btm  3'-AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[HY-BC x10]TTCGTCACCATAGTTGCGTCTCA[Tn5-BC x10]TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[==genomic==]-5'
read:                                  |1-10 HY | dark 11-33 (23nt) |34-43 Tn5| dark 44-62 (19nt) | 63-100 genomic |

library_sequencing.note: Primer anneals to the top-strand P7-adapter region (GAAGACAACAACTCCTTGGCTCACA), 3' end pointing into the index, and reads leftward using the top strand as template. It reports the third-round sample index as listed in Table S1 (e.g. TAGGTCCGAT), which is the reverse complement of the SAMPLE_INDEX shown on the top strand.
library_sequencing.diagram:
top  5'-...CCGAGCCCACGAGAC[idx x10]GAAGACAACAACTCCTTGGCTCACA-3'
btm  3'-...GGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[idx x10]CTTCTGTTGTTGAGGAACCGAGTGT-5'
pri              <-------3'-CTTCTGTTGTTGAGGAACCGAGTGT-5'   (reads index, 10 cyc)

library_sequencing.primer: Custom Tn5-modified (TM) Read 2 primer = Tn5 primer C oligo: GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG
library_sequencing.note: Primer is the reverse complement of the P7-arm top strand (Tn5-primer-C handle + ME), so it anneals to the top strand with its 3' ME end at the genomic boundary and reads the opposite end of the open-chromatin fragment leftward.
library_sequencing.diagram:
top  5'-[==genomic==]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[idx x10]GAAGACAACAACTCCTTGGCTCACA-3'
btm  3'-[==genomic==]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[idx x10]CTTCTGTTGTTGAGGAACCGAGTGT-5'
pri     <-----------3'-GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG-5'   (binds top, reads genomic, 100 cyc)

title: CH-ATAC-seq
description: Combinatorial-hybridization-based single-cell ATAC-seq (CH-ATAC-seq). It profiles single-nucleus chromatin accessibility (open chromatin / candidate cis-regulatory elements) by tagging open genomic DNA with a barcoded Tn5 transposase and then combinatorially indexing each nucleus in three rounds: (1) 384 barcoded Tn5 transposase adapters during tagmentation, (2) 768 ligase-free hybridization (HY) barcode oligos, and (3) 384 indexed MGI P7 PCR adapters during library amplification. Libraries are circularized and sequenced as DNA nanoballs on MGI DNBSEQ; the reads capture the two in-line combinatorial barcodes plus the genomic (accessible-chromatin) insert.

original_publication.title: Construction of single-cell cross-species chromatin accessibility landscapes with combinatorial-hybridization-based ATAC-seq

throughput.summary: High-throughput split-pool scATAC-seq handling hundreds of thousands of nuclei per experiment at below $0.01 per cell; ~550,000 nuclei profiled across three species.
throughput.cells: ~550,000 nuclei (145,485 zebrafish; 210,634 Drosophila; 193,971 Eisenia andrei); 852,180 nuclei across six species with mammalian integration
throughput.dna: ~1.1 million cCREs across the three focal species (union sets of 466,307 zebrafish; 117,002 Drosophila; 269,280 Eisenia andrei); ~0.8 million cell-type-specific cCREs across six species

publication.statistical_model: MACS2 (local-lambda Poisson model) for peak/cCRE calling; Wilcoxon rank-sum test (FDR <= 0.05) for cell-type-specific differentially accessible cCREs; phyloP (phylogenetic HMM) for conservation

other.label: Sequencer
other.value: MGI DNBSEQ-T7, dark-reaction model (read1 100 cyc with dark cycles 11-33 & 44-62; read2 100 cyc)

other.label: Collision rate
other.value: 0.5% for fresh vs 1.26% for fixed nuclei (HEK293T human / NIH-3T3 mouse mixing)

other.label: Key distinction vs sci-ATAC-seq3
other.value: Uses barcoded transposase for round 1 and a ligase-free hybridization reaction for round 2 (vs two ligation rounds)

data_processing.summary: Each library yields paired FASTQ files — one carrying the two in-line combinatorial indices (Tn5 384 + HY 768) at fixed base positions, the other carrying the genomic DNA read. Reads are demultiplexed by index matching, adapter-trimmed, aligned per species, Tn5-offset-corrected, deduplicated into a fragments file, then clustered and peak-called; cell-type-specific cCREs, motifs, cross-species conservation, and gene regulatory networks are derived downstream.
data_processing.statistical_model: MACS2 (Poisson local-lambda) for peak/cCRE calling; Wilcoxon rank-sum test (FDR <= 0.05) for differentially accessible cCREs; phyloP (phylogenetic HMM) for sequence conservation
